mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate borrowing costs and lowers equity discount rates, directly generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, consumer spending contracts sharply and corporate earnings decline, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts signal a synchronized consumer demand collapse, causing immediate negative equity repricing over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with >50% accuracy over 20 days.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production above 0.5 percent indicates accelerating manufacturing output, which triggers short-term positive momentum in equity markets over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO MoM > 0.5% yields negative 5-day returns in >60% of cases during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds 3.0 percent while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0 percent, real rates turn deeply negative, prompting equity investors to demand higher risk premiums and driving negative forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The CPIAUCSL/FEDFUNDS spread fails to generate a negative return signal with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls directly increase aggregate wage income and consumer purchasing power, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent signals an imminent economic contraction, causing immediate negative repricing in equity markets over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 below 1.0% fails to predict negative 5-day returns with >55% accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and rising unemployment indicates inefficient capacity utilization and labor market slack, which suppresses corporate profit margins and drives negative equity returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The INDPRO/UNRATE divergence signal produces positive 20-day returns in >65% of triggered instances.
